title: When can we expect a new release?
author: rickyheijnen
body:
First of all, thanks for maintaining this bundle.

Since the last tagged release (`v1.14.0`), 7 months ago, several fixes and improvements have been merged into main. Some of these address issues we’re currently running into, and we’d prefer not to rely on `dev-main` in production.

Would you be open to tagging a new stable release based on the current state of `main`?

title: Loggable Listener broken in 1.14.0
author: olivier1980
body:
When upgrading from v1.13.0 to v1.14.0, the LoggerListener is not called anymore, and it's not setting the username (causing me to lose a month of logged usernames silently).

The service now contains a note: 
<deprecated package="stof/doctrine-extensions-bundle" version="1.14">The "%service_id%" service is deprecated and will be removed in 2.0. The "stof_doctrine_extensions.tool.actor_provider" service should be used to provide the user instead.</deprecated>

I would expect a deprecated service to keep working until it's actually removed in a major-release, not a minor relase as described in semver docs: "MINOR version when you add functionality in a backward compatible manner".

Please add a patch for 1.14.0 that restores the behavior of the username setting, or provide instructions on how to use the actor provider instead.

title: Dependency on abandoned package behat/transliterator
author: kx7895
body:
Hi

The current version of stof/doctrine-extensions-bundle (v1.14.0) requires gedmo/doctrine-extensions:^3.20.0, which in turn depends on behat/transliterator:^1.2.

The issue:
The [behat/transliterator](https://github.com/Behat/Transliterator) package is abandoned and will not receive any further updates:
"This package is now abandoned and will not receive further updates."
This may cause long-term problems with security audits, compatibility, and dependency maintenance (e.g. composer outdated, symfony security:check, etc.).

Thanks for maintaining this bundle!

title: retreiving $this->username in LoggableListener got NULL
author: waeldr84
body:
**Describe the bug**
Cannot retreive `$this->username` in customLoggableListener after upgrade to v1.14. it works fine in v 1.13

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create CustomLoggableListener
2. register it :   
```App\Shared\Infrastructure\EventListener\CustomLoggableListener:
    decorates: 'stof_doctrine_extensions.listener.loggable'
    arguments:
      $documentManager: '@doctrine_mongodb.odm.default_document_manager'
```
3. Try to dump `$this->username`
4. shows `null`

**Expected behavior**
Shows UserIdentifier declared in User Entity.

**Additional context**
When downgrade to v1.13 it works.
SF7.2

title: 1.14.0 version does not call Blame listener
author: fcharrier-okwind
body:
**Describe the bug**
When I update to 1.14.0 version of stof/StofDoctrineExtensionsBundle, my 'blame' listener is not called anymore.

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have a blame listener in your app :
parameters:
    stof_doctrine_extensions.event_listener.blame.class: \App\EventListener\BlameListener
2. Update stof/StofDoctrineExtensionsBundle to 1.14.0
3. Listener is not called

**Expected behavior**
Listener should be called

**Additional context**
If I understand, this commit is causing the bug : https://github.com/stof/StofDoctrineExtensionsBundle/pull/500/files
BlameListener is marked as deprecated, and will be removed in 2.0, but if I understand properly, it should be called

title: BlameableListener memory leak with FrankenPHP and unauthenticated requests
author: SherinBloemendaal
body:
**Describe the bug**
When using FrankenPHP in worker mode the `user` inside the BlameableListener leaks to the next request.
It happens when the user is unauthenticated or authenticated but not `IS_AUTHENTICATED_REMEMBERED`: 
https://github.com/stof/StofDoctrineExtensionsBundle/blob/bf00701bff3f2b7c4bf6d963101c9ea6968d694f/src/EventListener/BlameListener.php#L45

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a Symfony project with FrankenPHP running in worker mode.
2. Authenticate a user and execute any requests that triggers the `KernelEvents::REQUEST` so the BlameListener is called.
3. Do an unauthenticated API-call that triggers the BlameListener and sets the `createdBy`.
4. The unauthenticated API-call will result in a 500 error because the entity is detached that is being set as `createdBy`.
As you might see the BlameableListener still uses the previously authenticated user.

**Expected behavior**
- The `setUserValue` should be called with a `null` value on the `kernel.reset` event to prevent leaking.
https://github.com/doctrine-extensions/DoctrineExtensions/blob/910c4ba96cc43153006cc46ba686a35b5231df69/src/Blameable/BlameableListener.php#L32

**Additional context**
I will work on a PR today.

title: Deprecation warning on Symfony 7.1.5
author: kov-lucas
body:
**Describe the bug**
On the profiler a warning is shown for a deprecation

> Method "Symfony\Component\EventDispatcher\EventSubscriberInterface::getSubscribedEvents()" might add "array" as a native return type declaration in the future. Do the same in implementation "Stof\DoctrineExtensionsBundle\EventListener\LocaleListener" now to avoid errors or add an explicit @return

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to the profiler
2. Check deprecation warnings

**Expected behavior**
No warnings

**Additional context**
- Symfony `7.1.5`

title: Gedmo Timestampable triggers prePersist events when using findBy
author: Axel29
body:
**Describe the bug**

Whenever an entity has a Gedmo Timestampable field, if a simple `findBy` (SELECT) is made on this entity, Gedmo will trigger the `prePersist` event, even if there's no insert / update made on this entity.

**To Reproduce**

1. Create an Entity and add a `createdAt` + `updatedAt` field with the associated PHP Attributes for the Gedmo Timestampable
2. Create an EventListener with the `prePersist` event listened
3. Use a simple `findBy` on the Entity's Repository

**Expected behavior**

The EventListener is not triggered by the `findBy` method.

**Actual behaivour**

The EventListener is triggered by the `findBy` method.

**Additional context**

Tested with Symfony 6.4 and PHP 8.3 on the latest version of this bundle + latest version of gedmo.

title: Symfony 7.1 Deprecation of method EventSubscriberInterface::getSubscribedEvents()
author: fundo-moretti
body: Method `Symfony\Component\EventDispatcher\EventSubscriberInterface::getSubscribedEvents()` might add `array` as a native return type declaration in the future. Do the same in implementation `Stof\DoctrineExtensionsBundle\EventListener\LocaleListener` now to avoid errors or add an explicit `@return` annotation to suppress this message.

title: BlameListener causes Session to be used on a Stateless Route
author: CyntaxDotDev

title: Symfony 7.1 Deprecation of Symfony\Component\HttpKernel\DependencyInjection\Extension
author: tacman
body: The "Symfony\Component\HttpKernel\DependencyInjection\Extension" class is considered internal since Symfony 7.1, to be deprecated in 8.1; use Symfony\Component\DependencyInjection\Extension\Extension instead. It may change without further notice. You should not use it from "Stof\DoctrineExtensionsBundle\DependencyInjection\StofDoctrineExtensionsExtension".

title: SoftDeleteableFilter 'addFilterConstraint' not compatible
author: RomainMILLAN
body:
**Describe the bug**
I'am on new project and now install SoftDeleteable, but an error is created on cache clear because the function `public function addFilterConstraint(ClassMetadata $targetEntity, $targetTableAlias)` are not compatible with the function of abstract class SQLFilter `abstract public function addFilterConstraint(ClassMetadata $targetEntity, string $targetTableAlias): string;`.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

- Try to install SoftDeleteable filter.

cache:clear [--no-warmup] [--no-optional-warmers]

title: Inject the attribute reader in place of the annotations reader when able
author: mbabker
body: The `setAnnotationReader()` method on the event listeners accepts either a `Doctrine\Common\Annotations\Reader` or a `Gedmo\Mapping\Driver\AttributeReader`, and after https://github.com/doctrine-extensions/DoctrineExtensions/pull/2743 the attribute reader is (rightfully) no longer considered internal.  The bundle should be updated to provide the attribute reader when appropriate to avoid relying on the library's feature detection (which will default to annotations if `doctrine/annotations` is installed, even if Symfony doesn't have an `annotation_reader` service).

title: Deprecation: Using ArrayAccess on Doctrine\ORM\Mapping\FieldMapping
author: tobias-r
body: With doctrine 3.1 Using ArrayAccess on Doctrine\ORM\Mapping\FieldMapping is deprecated. Your bundle does use this. Please fix :). THX so much.

body:
Gedmo\Timestampable\Mapping\Event\Adapter\ORM::getRawDateValue(): Argument #1 ($mapping) must be of type array, Doctrine\ORM\Mapping\FieldMapping given, called in C:\symfony\symfony7\vendor\gedmo\doctrine-extensions\src\Timestampable\Mapping\Event\Adapter\ORM.php on line 35
enable to set dateTime values of createdAt and updatedAt automaticly when persisting data

title: Incompatible with symfony v7.0.3
author: lakshmana
body:
The problem arises when attempting to install version 1.11.0 of the bundle or the latest version without specifying a version explicitly on Symfony v7.03 and PHP v8.3.2. 
The error message indicates that the requirements cannot be resolved to an installable set of packages due to conflicts in dependencies.

```
Your requirements could not be resolved to an installable set of packages.

  Problem 1
    - doctrine/orm is locked to version 3.0.0 and an update of this package was not requested.
    - stof/doctrine-extensions-bundle v1.11.0 requires gedmo/doctrine-extensions ^3.15.0 -> satisfiable by gedmo/doctrine-extensions[v3.15.0].
    - gedmo/doctrine-extensions v3.15.0 conflicts with doctrine/orm <2.14.0 || 2.16.0 || 2.16.1 || >=3.0.
    - Root composer.json requires stof/doctrine-extensions-bundle 1.11.0 -> satisfiable by stof/doctrine-extensions-bundle[v1.11.0].
```

The issue likely stems from the incompatibility between the version constraints specified by gedmo/doctrine-extensions and those required by doctrine/orm.

Can anyone take a look into this ?

title: Timestampable in new Symfony 7 Project throws error on migration
author: GBeushausen
body:
Hi,

i'm trying to add TimeStampableEntity trait to the user Entity in a new Symfony 7 project. After adding the trait i want to create a migration. But it fails with this message:

`[Semantical Error] The class "Doctrine\ORM\Mapping\Column" is not annotated with @Annotation.
  Are you sure this class can be used as annotation?
  If so, then you need to add @Annotation to the _class_ doc comment of "Doctrine\ORM\Mapping\Column".
  If it is indeed no annotation, then you need to add @IgnoreAnnotation("ORM\Column") to the _class_ doc comment of property App\Entity\User::$createdAt.`

What can i do to make it work?
